title: POST /notes accepts missing title, violating documented schema
body:
## Description
`POST /notes` silently accepts a payload with no `title` field and returns
`201`, storing the note as-is. The README documents the create schema as
`{title, body?, tags?}` â€” `title` has no `?`, meaning it's required â€” but
neither `src/server.mjs` nor `src/store.mjs` enforce that. This is the root
cause behind a full `/search` outage (see the companion ticket, "GET
/search 500s for the whole collection if any note lacks a title"): any note
with `title === undefined` reaches `searchNotes()` and blows up.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`NoteStore.create()` in `src/store.mjs:26` destructures the payload as
`{ title, body = "", tags = [] }` â€” `body` and `tags` get defaults but
`title` does not, so a missing `title` becomes `undefined` and is stored
verbatim. The HTTP handler in `src/server.mjs:28-30` passes the parsed
request body straight to `store.create(body)` with no validation step in
between.

## File Pointers
- `src/server.mjs:28-30` â€” POST /notes handler, no validation before `store.create(body)`
- `src/store.mjs:26` â€” `create({ title, body = "", tags = [] })`, `title` has no default/guard
- `README.md:16` â€” documents `{title, body?, tags?}` as the create schema (title required)

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server (`buildApp()` or `node src/server.mjs`).
2. `POST /notes` with body `{"body": "no title"}` (no `title` key at all).
3. Observe: `201` with the note stored, `title: undefined` in the response â€”
   the README's schema is violated with no error.

## Expected Behavior
`POST /notes` with a missing or empty `title` returns `400` with a clear
error body (matching the existing convention where the server already
returns the parse error's `err.message` on JSON parse failures).

## Actual Behavior
Returns `201` and stores a note with `title: undefined`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /notes` with no `title` key returns `400` with an error body
- [ ] `POST /notes` with `title: ""` (empty string) returns `400`
- [ ] `POST /notes` with a valid non-empty `title` still returns `201` as today
- [ ] `scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs` test 1
      ("author builds a realistic note collection, including one bad
      payload") goes green
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering the 400 case

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 1)
- Tier: T2

## Notes
Root cause of the /search 500 outage â€” see the companion ticket for that
symptom. Fixing this ticket alone prevents any new title-less note from
being created; the companion ticket hardens search.mjs itself.
notes:
Related: 17846222216546600150 (GET /search 500s for the whole collection if any note lacks a title) is the downstream symptom of this root cause.
Fix for this ticket was closed/abandoned without merging (PR #1, https://github.com/HassaanSaleem/kitchenloop-demo/pull/1, branch kitchen/batch-notes-validation-search-share deleted, mergedAt: null). Bug is still live on main. Re-promoting for execution.
Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.
Fix PR #2 (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening) was closed without merging (closed 2026-07-21T11:48:55Z: "superseded â€” re-running with the full reviewer tribunal enabled", per the harness fixes in d597909). Bug/gap is still live on main. Re-promoting to todo by Kitchen Loop backlog grooming (iteration 1).
Picked up by Kitchen Loop execute (iteration 1) in bundle kitchen/batch-notes-hardening; moved to in_progress.

title: GET /search 500s for the whole collection if any note lacks a title
body:
## Description
A single note whose `title` is `undefined` (see companion validation
ticket) breaks `GET /search` for every caller, not just for that one note â€”
the endpoint returns `500` instead of skipping or gracefully handling the
malformed record.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`searchNotes()` in `src/search.mjs:6` calls
`note.title.includes(query) || note.body.includes(query)` unconditionally
for every note in the store. If any note's `title` is `undefined` (which
`POST /notes` currently allows â€” see companion ticket), `.includes` throws
`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading includes)`,
which propagates up through `src/server.mjs`'s generic `catch` block
(`server.mjs:59-60`) as a `500` for the entire request, taking down search
results for every well-formed note too.

## File Pointers
- `src/search.mjs:6` â€” `note.title.includes(query) || note.body.includes(query)`, no null/undefined guard
- `src/server.mjs:45-46` â€” `GET /search` route, delegates straight to `searchNotes()`
- `src/server.mjs:59-60` â€” generic `catch` turns the thrown `TypeError` into a `500`

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` with `{"title": "Groceries", "body": "milk, eggs"}`.
3. `POST /notes` with `{"body": "no title"}` (missing `title`; accepted today â€” see companion ticket).
4. `GET /search?q=milk`.
5. Observe: `500 {"error": "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading includes)"}` instead of finding "Groceries".

## Expected Behavior
`GET /search` returns matching notes and tolerates any note with a missing
`title` or `body`, never 500s because of one malformed record.

## Actual Behavior
The whole endpoint 500s until the offending note is deleted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GET /search` no longer throws when a note has `title === undefined` (or `body === undefined`)
- [ ] Search still correctly matches well-formed notes in the same collection as a malformed one
- [ ] `scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs` test 2
      ("search must not 500 for the whole collection because of one bad
      note") goes green
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering search against a mixed (good + malformed) collection

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 2)
- Tier: T2

## Notes
Downstream symptom of the missing-title-validation bug (companion ticket)
â€” fixing that ticket stops new malformed notes from being created, but
this ticket is the defensive fix in search.mjs itself so search is
robust regardless of how a malformed note got into the store.
notes:

title: POST /notes/:id/share returns 201 for a note id that doesn't exist
body:
## Description
`POST /notes/:id/share` mints and returns a valid-looking `{token}` even
when `:id` was never created (or was already deleted), instead of `404`
like every other "operate on an unknown note id" path in this API.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`ShareRegistry.share()` in `src/share.mjs:10-14` unconditionally generates
a token and maps it to `noteId` with no existence check. The route handler
in `src/server.mjs:48-50` calls `shares.share(shareMatch[1])` directly,
never calling `store.get(shareMatch[1])` first â€” unlike the sibling
`GET /notes/:id` (`server.mjs:37-39`) and `DELETE /notes/:id`
(`server.mjs:41-43`) handlers, which both check existence and 404
correctly.

## File Pointers
- `src/server.mjs:48-50` â€” POST /notes/:id/share handler, no `store.get()` existence check
- `src/server.mjs:37-39`, `src/server.mjs:41-43` â€” sibling handlers that correctly 404 on unknown id (the pattern to match)
- `src/share.mjs:10-14` â€” `ShareRegistry.share(noteId)`, never validates `noteId`

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes/does-not-exist/share`.
3. Observe: `201 {"token": "<hex>"}` instead of `404`.
4. `GET /shared/<token>` correctly 404s (confirms the token is a
   "successful"-looking but permanently dead share).

## Expected Behavior
`POST /notes/:id/share` returns `404 {"error": "not found"}` for an id that
doesn't resolve to an existing note, matching `GET /notes/:id` and
`DELETE /notes/:id`.

## Actual Behavior
Returns `201` with a token that will never resolve to real data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /notes/:id/share` returns `404` when `store.get(id)` is null
- [ ] `POST /notes/:id/share` still returns `201 {token}` for a real note id
- [ ] `scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs` test 3
      ("sharing a note id that was never created should 404, not 201")
      goes green
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering the 404 case

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 3)
- Tier: T2

title: Search is case-sensitive; should match regardless of case
body:
## Description
`GET /search?q=relay` does not match a note titled "Relay Launch" because
matching is a plain case-sensitive substring check. Every comparable
note-search UI treats search as case-insensitive by default, so this reads
as a bug to any real user even though nothing documents case-sensitivity
either way.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`searchNotes()` in `src/search.mjs:6` does a raw `.includes(query)` against
`note.title`/`note.body` with no case normalization on either side.

## File Pointers
- `src/search.mjs:3-8` â€” `searchNotes(store, query)`, the whole matching function

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` with `{"title": "Relay Launch", "body": "..."}`.
3. `GET /search?q=relay`.
4. Observe: `[]` â€” no match, even though "Relay" appears in the title.

## Expected Behavior
`GET /search?q=relay` matches "Relay Launch" regardless of the query's or
the note's casing.

## Actual Behavior
Case must match exactly for a substring hit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GET /search` matches notes regardless of query/note casing (e.g. `q=relay` matches "Relay Launch")
- [ ] Existing exact-case search behavior continues to work (no regression)
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering a mixed-case query

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (case-sensitivity probe)
- Tier: T2

Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.

title: Search never looks at a note's tags, only title/body
body:
## Description
Notes can be tagged at creation time (`POST /notes {tags: [...]}`), but
`GET /search` never looks at `tags` â€” searching for text that is literally
one of a note's tags returns nothing unless that same text also happens to
appear in the title or body. Tags are effectively write-only today.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`searchNotes()` in `src/search.mjs:5-7` filters only on
`note.title.includes(query) || note.body.includes(query)` â€” `note.tags` is
never consulted.

## File Pointers
- `src/search.mjs:3-8` â€” `searchNotes(store, query)`, the whole matching function
- `src/store.mjs:26` â€” confirms `tags` is a first-class field on every note

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` with `{"title": "Standup notes", "body": "...", "tags": ["work"]}`.
3. `GET /search?q=work`.
4. Observe: `[]` â€” the tag isn't matched even though it's an exact tag value.

## Expected Behavior
`GET /search` also matches against a note's `tags` array (exact or
substring match on any tag), in addition to `title`/`body`.

## Actual Behavior
Only `title`/`body` are searched; `tags` are ignored entirely.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `GET /search?q=<tag>` returns notes where `<tag>` matches an entry in `tags`
- [ ] Existing title/body matching continues to work unchanged
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering a tag-only match

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (tag-search probe)
- Tier: T2

Re-executed in iteration 1 bundle (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening -> PR #2, commit eaf1d31). Oracle green; UAT gate PASS 8/8.
Fix PR #2 (branch kitchen/batch-notes-hardening) was closed without merging (closed 2026-07-21T11:48:55Z: "superseded â€” re-running with the full reviewer tribunal enabled", per the harness fixes in d597909). title: No way to list or revoke a note's active share tokens
body:
## Description
`POST /notes/:id/share` can be called any number of times and every token
it has ever issued stays valid until the note itself is deleted â€” there is
no endpoint to see what tokens exist for a note or to revoke one
individually. For a feature documented as a "read-only share link", not
being able to revoke a single leaked/stale link without deleting the
underlying note (destroying the author's own content) is a real gap.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`ShareRegistry` in `src/share.mjs` only exposes `share()` (mint) and
`resolve()` (read); there's no `listByNote()`/`revoke()` and no route in
`src/server.mjs` for either. This isn't a bug â€” nothing currently documents
otherwise â€” but it's a missing capability confirmed by the iteration-1
scenario (sharing the same note twice mints two independently valid
tokens, both permanently live).

## File Pointers
- `src/share.mjs:5-19` â€” `ShareRegistry` class; needs a way to enumerate/revoke tokens for a given `noteId`
- `src/server.mjs:48-51` â€” where a new `GET /notes/:id/shares` and/or `DELETE /notes/:id/share/:token`-style route would live, alongside the existing share route

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server.
2. `POST /notes` to create a note, then `POST /notes/:id/share` twice.
3. Observe: two independently valid tokens exist for the same note, with no
   endpoint to list or revoke either one short of deleting the note.

## Expected Behavior
An author can (a) list the outstanding share tokens for a note, and (b)
revoke an individual token, without deleting the note itself.

## Actual Behavior
The only way to invalidate any share link is to delete the note, which
also destroys the note's content for the author.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] An endpoint exists to list a note's active share tokens
- [ ] An endpoint exists to revoke a single token without deleting the note
- [ ] A revoked token's `GET /shared/:token` 404s immediately, matching the existing delete-note revocation behavior
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering list + revoke

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/notes-search-share-seams/scenario.mjs (test 5, informational)
- Tier: T2
notes: Related: new ticket 17846373851918724608 ("Share tokens do not survive a service restart", iteration 1 Round 2) documents the same root-cause gap (share tokens have no durable, inspectable home) from the opposite direction â€” tokens vanishing involuntarily vs. not being revocable voluntarily.

title: Share tokens do not survive a service restart
body:
## Description
`POST /notes/:id/share` mints a share token that is only ever stored in
`ShareRegistry`'s in-memory `Map` â€” it is never written to disk. Any service
restart (deploy, crash recovery, `npm start` re-run) resets `ShareRegistry`
to empty, so `GET /shared/:token` starts returning `404` for every share
link issued before that restart, even though `NoteStore` correctly persists
the underlying notes across the exact same restart. A reader with a
bookmarked/emailed share link has no way to know whether the link died
because the author deleted the note (working as intended) or because the
service merely restarted (a bug) â€” both look identical: a bare
`404 {"error": "not found"}`.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`ShareRegistry` (`src/share.mjs:6-14`) keeps its token -> noteId map in a
plain in-memory `Map` with no file I/O at all, unlike `NoteStore`
(`src/store.mjs`), which persists every note to `data/notes.json` and
reloads it on construction. `buildApp()` constructs a fresh `NoteStore` and
a fresh `ShareRegistry` every time it's called â€” which is exactly what
happens on a real process restart or redeploy, so the note survives but
the token map does not.

## File Pointers
- `src/share.mjs:6-14` â€” `ShareRegistry` class; token -> noteId `Map` has no persistence
- `src/store.mjs` â€” sibling class that already solves this problem for notes (persist-on-write, reload-on-construct); the pattern to mirror
- `src/server.mjs` â€” `buildApp()` construction site where `ShareRegistry` is instantiated fresh each call

## Reproduction Steps
1. Start the server (`buildApp()` or `node src/server.mjs`).
2. `POST /notes` to create a note.
3. `POST /notes/:id/share` to mint a token; confirm `GET /shared/:token` is `200`.
4. Restart the process (or, in-process, construct a new `buildApp({ storePath })` against the same store path).
5. `GET /shared/:token` with the same token.
6. Observe: `404`, despite `GET /notes/:id` still returning `200` for the same note (data itself is untouched).

## Expected Behavior
A share token minted before a restart continues to resolve after the
restart, exactly as the underlying note itself does.

## Actual Behavior
Every share token issued before a restart 404s afterward, indistinguishable
from the note having been deleted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Share tokens resolve correctly across a service restart (e.g. persisted the same way `NoteStore` persists notes)
- [ ] A token minted, then the service restarted, then resolved via `GET /shared/:token` still returns `200` with the correct note
- [ ] `scenarios/incubating/share-tokens-restart-durability/scenario.mjs` test 3 ("a share token does NOT survive a service restart, even though the note does") goes green
- [ ] Regression test added under `tests/*.test.mjs` covering token durability across a store/registry reconstruction

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md (Round 2)
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/share-tokens-restart-durability/scenario.mjs (test 3)
- Tier: T2

## Notes
Related: the existing "No way to list or revoke a note's active share
tokens" ticket is about the opposite direction (tokens can't be revoked
voluntarily) â€” both point at the same root cause: share tokens have no
durable, inspectable home. Fixing this ticket (persistence) should share
the same on-disk mechanism a future list/revoke feature would use.

title: No way to tell why a share link stopped working (restart vs. deletion)
body:
## Description
When a share link stops resolving (`GET /shared/:token` returns `404`),
there is no error message, log line, or response header anywhere that
tells a reader or the author *why* â€” whether the author deleted the note
(intended revocation) or the service simply restarted (see the companion
restart-durability ticket). Both cases return an identical bare
`404 {"error": "not found"}`, so a previously-working link going dark is
indistinguishable from a bug versus expected behavior, from either side of
the API.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
`GET /shared/:token` (`src/server.mjs`) returns the same generic 404 body
for every token-resolution failure, regardless of cause â€” there is no
signal in `ShareRegistry` or the response for "this token never existed
this could be a lost mapping" as opposed to "this note was deliberately
deleted."

## File Pointers
- `src/server.mjs` â€” `GET /shared/:token` handler, single generic 404 path for all resolution failures
- `src/share.mjs` â€” `ShareRegistry.resolve()`, has no way to distinguish "never existed" from "existed, now gone"

## Reproduction Steps
1. `POST /notes`, `POST /notes/:id/share`, confirm `GET /shared/:token` is `200`.
2. Either restart the service, or `DELETE /notes/:id` the underlying note.
3. `GET /shared/:token` in both cases.
4. Observe: identical `404 {"error": "not found"}` response body in both scenarios â€” no way for a caller to tell them apart.

## Expected Behavior
A caller can distinguish "this share link was intentionally revoked
(note deleted)" from "this share link died for an infrastructure reason
(e.g. registry reset)" â€” at minimum this should be documented as a known
limitation; ideally the response or logs carry a distinguishing signal.

## Actual Behavior
Both cases return the exact same generic 404 with no distinguishing
information.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document the current behavior (or add a distinguishing signal) so a reader/author is not left guessing why a link died
- [ ] No change to the existing `200`/`404` contract for well-formed cases (this is additive/documentation, not a breaking change)

## Source
- Iteration: 1
- Report: docs/internal/reports/iteration-1-report.md (Round 2)
- Scenario: scenarios/incubating/share-tokens-restart-durability/scenario.mjs
- Tier: T2

## Notes
Related to "Share tokens do not survive a service restart" â€” that ticket
fixes the restart case directly (so this ambiguity mostly disappears for
that specific cause), but the underlying messaging gap is independent and
would still apply to any other cause of token loss (e.g. a corrupted
persisted registry file, once one exists).
